title: ANALYTICAL CHEMISTRY OF THORIUM. PART VI. SEPARATION FROM RARE EARTHS. CAMPHORIC ACID
author: D. S. N. MURTY
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: It is shown that camphoric acid precipitates thorium quantitatively in an ammonium acetate buffer. The approximate  p H has been determined as 4.2 and higher. Cerite earths are precipitated by the reagent at  p H 6.2 and higher. Separation of thorium from the cerite earths has been achieved by a double precipitation at  p H 4.4. The procedure has been utilised for the estimation of thorium in monazite extracts. The behaviour of a few other elements Ni, Co, Ti, etc., towards the reagent has also investigated.
steps_raw: It is shown that camphoric acid precipitates thorium quantitatively in an ammonium acetate buffer. The approximate  p H has been determined as 4.2 and higher. Cerite earths are precipitated by the reagent at  p H 6.2 and higher. Separation of thorium from the cerite earths has been achieved by a double precipitation at  p H 4.4. The procedure has been utilised for the estimation of thorium in monazite extracts. The behaviour of a few other elements Ni, Co, Ti, etc., towards the reagent has also investigated.
